description:
What: The Cheney tracer (runtime/src/heap.rs cheney_trace_children) has hand-written per-HeapKind arms. Where the arm only iterates the schema's FzValue fields, fold into the generic FieldKind-driven loop. Keep arms that have genuinely special structure (List spine, Closure captures).

Why: New HeapKinds inherit tracing for free; removes boilerplate.

CLIF impact: runtime-only; no JIT CLIF impact.

Removes: repeated per-HeapKind tracer boilerplate.

Tests: existing GC tests pass under stress.

comments.text:
**Audit complete — no foldable arms.**

`cheney_trace_children` (runtime/src/heap.rs:1018) arms:
- `Struct` — already uses `FieldKind::FzValue` walk over `schema.fields` (the generic pattern; this IS the target shape).
- `List` — hardcoded 2-slot walk (head@+16, tail@+24). No schema; computed layout. Cannot fold without giving List a synthetic schema.
- `Closure` — hardcoded captures walk (count from `flags`, slots at +24+i*8). No schema; layout derived from runtime flags. Cannot fold without dynamic schema construction.
- `Map` — hardcoded key/value pairs walk (count at +16, pairs at +24+i*16). No schema; computed layout.
- `Bitstring | Float | VecI64 | VecF64 | VecU8 | VecBit | ProcBin` — already collapsed into a single "raw payload, no children" arm.

The hand-coded arms (List/Closure/Map) have genuine layout specificity that the schema-driven walk doesn't express today. Folding them would require either (a) registering synthetic schemas for non-Struct HeapKinds or (b) a separate "layout walker" abstraction — both bigger than E3's stated scope.

Recommendation: close as audit-complete, no code changes. Revisit if/when List/Closure/Map gain schema representation for unrelated reasons.

title: fz-dps.E3: collapse per-HeapKind tracer arms into FieldKind-driven loop
